jd_text: Job Description The Executive Director - Local Content will spearhead strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing the utilization of local resources and fostering sustainable development within the organization and its operational communities. This role demands a visionary leader who can advance local content policies and practices, ensuring alignment with both national and organizational goals. The Executive Director will be responsible for cultivating partnerships with local stakeholders, driving economic diversification, and integrating best practices in local sourcing. The ideal candidate will possess exceptional skills in stakeholder engagement, policy development, and project management, alongside the acumen to analyze market trends and forecast long-term local content requirements. This position requires a deep understanding of local content regulations, impacts on community relations, and the ability to navigate complex operational landscapes effectively. Job Requirements A minimum of 15 years of progressive leadership experience in local content development, strategy formulation, and operational management, preferably within the industry sector. Proven track record of successful implementation of local content policies and practices, resulting in measurable benefits to local economies. Demonstrated experience in engaging with governmental bodies, industry groups, and local communities to facilitate productive partnerships. Strong analytical capability to assess local market conditions, resources, and expertise to inform strategic decision-making. Advanced degree in Business Administration, Economics, Environmental Studies, or a related field. Exceptional negotiation and conflict resolution skills, with a history of handling sensitive negotiations that yield consensus among diverse stakeholder groups. Proficiency in project management methodologies and tools, including program evaluations and impact assessments. Comprehensive understanding of global best practices in local content development, supply chain optimization, and community engagement. Experience in leading cross-functional teams and managing multi-disciplinary projects that require collaboration across various organizational levels. Strong financial acumen with experience in budgeting, financial forecasting, and economic impact assessments. Job Responsibilities Develop and implement a comprehensive local content strategy that aligns with the organization's objectives and compliance with regulatory frameworks. Lead the formulation of key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure the effectiveness of local content initiatives and adapt strategies as necessary based on data-driven insights. Engage with key stakeholders, including governmental agencies, local businesses, and community leaders, to advocate for and advance local content opportunities. Analyze and report on local content market trends, identifying opportunities for growth and addressing challenges in local sourcing. Facilitate training and development programs aimed at enhancing the skills and capabilities of local workforce and suppliers. Oversee the integration of local content practices into the organization’s supply chain management processes to maximize local economic benefits. Collaborate with senior leadership to align local content strategies with broader corporate sustainability goals and initiatives. Monitor and ensure compliance with local content regulations and reporting requirements, preparing detailed compliance reports for internal and external stakeholders. Serve as the organization's representative in industry forums, workshops, and conferences focused on local content and sustainable development practices. Drive constructive dialogue and relationships with indigenous communities and local interest groups to foster trust and gain insights for strategic planning. Required Skills Strategic vision and the ability to think critically about long-term local content and sustainability initiatives. Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present complex information clearly to diverse audiences. Strong interpersonal skills to build rapport and effectively manage stakeholder relationships. High-level problem-solving capabilities with a capacity for innovative thinking and adaptability in dynamic environments. Proficient in data analysis tools and methodologies to inform decision-making processes. Leadership abilities that inspire teams, promoting collaboration and accountability across all levels of the organization. Strong administrative and operational capabilities to manage budgets, resources, and project timelines efficiently. Firm understanding of international standards and ethics related to local content and community engagement. Ability to work effectively in a multicultural and interdisciplinary environment, with a solid commitment to diversity and inclusion. Resilience and determination to champion local content efforts, overcoming any barriers that may arise in implementing initiatives.
